revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Sawi-qed-seret
''{{unicode|Qd-b3.wj}}''
lies: ''S3.wj qd''
''Kinder (Seelen) des Schafs unter dem Schaf'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der zwei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Sterne]] umfasste und sich in unmittelbarer Nähe zum altägyptischen [[Sternbild]] [[Schaf (Sternbild)|Schaf]] befand, welches in vielen Särgen auf den [[Diagonalsternuhr]]en und beispielsweise auch im Grab des [[Senenmut]] abgebildet ist. Der auffälligste [[Stern]] ist hierbei [[Sirrah|Alpheratz]] im Sternbild [[Andromeda (Sternbild)|Andromeda]]. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sawi-seret am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den 28. Dekan. Der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang war für den [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|6. Peret II]] angesetzt und hatte als Datierungsgrundlage die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 92. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 396. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Sawi-qed-seret

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:サウィ・ケド・セレト]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Sawi-qed-seret]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. ''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. ''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|de|Sirrah}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|de|Andromeda (Sternbild)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|qq=draft}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Sawi-qed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] }} [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[ja:サウィ・ケド・セレト]] [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Peret II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Sawi-qed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] }} [[sn:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[ja:サウィ・ケド・セレト]] [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q1402958}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1402958]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is [[:en:Alpheratz|Alpheratz]] in the constellation [[:en:Andromeda (constellation)|Andromeda (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 396. {{translated page|de|Sawi-qed-seret|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:サウィ・ケド・セレト]] [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q1402958}}

[[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1402958]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:25, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=}}s and, for example, in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]. The most prominent [[:en:Star|Star]] associated with this decan is [[:en:Alpheratz|Alpheratz]] in the constellation [[:en:Andromeda (constellation)|Andromeda (constellation)]]. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] [[de:Sawi-qed-seret]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = Aa28-Aa28-E11-E11 N14-N33-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Sawi-qed-seret
''Qd-b3.wj-sr.t''
read as: ''S3.wj-qd-sr.t''
''Children (Souls) of the Sheep beneath the Sheep'' }} '''Sawi-qed-seret''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was located in close proximity to the Ancient Egyptian {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation|1=Constellation}} {{ill|Sheep (constellation)|de|Schaf (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sheep (constellation)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, depicted in many coffins on {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|WD=Q142353|1=Star clock}}s and, for example, in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|en|Senenmut|WD=Q328732|lt=Senenmut|1=Senenmut}}. The most prominent {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} associated with this decan is {{ill|Alpheratz|en|Alpheratz|WD=Q13039|lt=Alpheratz|1=Alpheratz}} in the constellation {{ill|Andromeda (constellation)|en|Andromeda (constellation)|WD=Q9256|lt=Andromeda (constellation)|1=Andromeda (constellation)}}. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Sawi-qed-seret represented the 28th decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}. The {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the {{ill|Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|lt=6th Peret II|de|6. Peret II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == See also == * [[Sawi-seret]] == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 92. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''.
